name: HCP Terraform
description: Managed Terraform platform (formerly Terraform Cloud) for provisioning infrastructure through version-controlled workspaces with policy-as-code guardrails. Evidence covers workspace run history and apply approvals as change management records, Sentinel/OPA policy sets, team access permissions, state version history and the organisation audit trail.

L1.title: HCP Terraform Manual Run History and Access Evidence Collection

steps.action: Sign in to app.terraform.io, open each in-scope workspace and select Runs; capture the run list (status, source, triggering user, timestamps), then open a sample applied run to capture the plan summary, the approver recorded against the apply confirmation and the completion timestamp
steps.permissions_required: Team access of at least Read on the workspace
steps.audit_note: A run that required manual confirmation before apply is direct change management evidence — the plan is the proposed change and the confirming user is the approver

steps.action: Navigate to Organization Settings > Policy Sets and capture each policy set's kind (Sentinel or OPA), enforcement level, scope (global or selected workspaces/projects) and source repository
steps.audit_note: Policy set scope and enforcement levels evidence preventative guardrails applied to infrastructure changes before apply

steps.action: Navigate to Organization Settings > Teams to capture team membership and organisation-level permissions, then open each in-scope workspace's Settings > Team Access to capture the access level (read, plan, write, admin or custom) granted to each team
steps.permissions_required: Organization Owner, or Workspace Admin for the per-workspace team access view
steps.audit_note: Team access levels are the authoritative record of who can queue plans, approve applies and administer each workspace

steps.action: Open each in-scope workspace's States page and capture the state version history — serial numbers, creation timestamps and the run or user that produced each version
steps.permissions_required: Team access including read state versions on the workspace
steps.security_note: Capture the version list metadata only — do not download raw state files for evidence, as state can contain plaintext secrets
steps.audit_note: State version history corroborates that infrastructure changes only occurred through recorded runs

steps.action: Navigate to Organization Settings > API Token and record which organisation, audit trail and team tokens exist, when they were created and their expiry dates
steps.security_note: Record token metadata only — token values are shown once at creation and must never appear in evidence
steps.audit_note: A token inventory with enforced expiry dates evidences credential lifecycle management for the platform's API access

steps.action: Upload the screenshots and exports to the evidence repository with date-stamped filenames and evidence ID references
steps.security_note: Review captures for exposed variable values or state contents before uploading

L2.title: HCP Terraform Notification-Driven Run Records with Scheduled Reviews
L2.estimated_time: 1 hour initial setup, then 15 minutes per cycle

steps.action: HCP Terraform has no built-in scheduled evidence report or export feature — the closest reliable alternative is workspace notifications delivering a record of every run outcome, combined with a scheduled manual export of configuration evidence
steps.audit_note: Documenting this design decision evidences a deliberate, repeatable collection process rather than ad hoc screenshots

steps.action: In each in-scope workspace, navigate to Settings > Notifications and create an email notification to a monitored compliance mailbox, triggered on the Needs Attention, Completed and Errored run events
steps.audit_note: Each notification is a vendor-timestamped record of a run outcome, including runs that required human confirmation before apply

steps.action: Retain the notification emails in a retention-managed mailbox or archive folder so the full period's run outcomes accumulate automatically between review cycles
steps.security_note: Restrict the compliance mailbox to authorised personnel — run notifications reveal workspace names and change activity
steps.audit_note: An append-only mail archive gives a continuous, independently timestamped run record without custom code

steps.action: On a recurring calendar schedule, export the slower-moving configuration evidence — Organization Settings > Policy Sets, Organization Settings > Teams and each workspace's Settings > Team Access — following the L1 steps
steps.audit_note: Policy sets and team access change infrequently, so a monthly snapshot cadence is proportionate provided each snapshot is date-stamped

steps.action: File the period's run notification archive and configuration snapshots in the evidence repository, and record each cycle's completion and period coverage in the evidence tracker
steps.audit_note: Reconciling notification counts against the workspace Runs page confirms no run records were missed during the period

steps.action: Create the collection credentials in Organization Settings > API Token: generate an organisation API token for configuration and run collection, and a separate audit trail token (under the Audit Token heading) for the audit trail endpoint, each with an explicit expiry date
steps.security_note: Token values are displayed once — store them immediately in your secrets manager, never in scripts or CI variables in plaintext, and diarise rotation before the mandatory expiry date

steps.action: Schedule the collection script to call GET https://app.terraform.io/api/v2/organizations/:organization_name/runs (with page[size] and filter[status] parameters) to capture run history across workspaces, storing the JSON responses date-stamped; each run's status, created-by and timestamps form the change record
steps.audit_note: Run records showing a plan followed by a human-confirmed apply are machine-readable change management evidence, filterable by status and source

steps.action: Collect access and policy configuration in the same run: GET /api/v2/organizations/:organization_name/policy-sets for Sentinel/OPA policy sets and their enforcement scope, GET /api/v2/organizations/:organization_name/teams for team membership, and GET /api/v2/team-workspaces?filter[workspace][id]=:workspace_id for per-workspace access levels
steps.audit_note: The team-workspaces response records the exact access level (read, plan, write, admin or custom) per team per workspace — the authoritative permissions evidence

steps.action: Capture state lineage with GET /api/v2/state-versions?filter[organization][name]=:org&filter[workspace][name]=:workspace, recording serial, created-at and status per version but discarding the hosted-state-download-url without fetching it
steps.permissions_required: Organisation API token with read state versions permission on the workspaces
steps.security_note: Never download raw state into the evidence store — state files can contain plaintext credentials; the version metadata alone evidences change lineage

steps.action: Pull the organisation audit trail with GET https://app.terraform.io/api/v2/organization/audit-trail?since=<last-run-ISO8601> using the audit trail token; note this endpoint requires the HCP Terraform Standard or Premium edition and is not available on lower tiers — if unavailable, document run history plus notifications as the compensating record
steps.permissions_required: Audit trail token (organisation or user tokens are not accepted)
steps.audit_note: HCP Terraform retains only 14 days of audit trail data, so daily collection is required to build a gap-free archive

steps.action: Implement error handling and alerting for failed runs — 401/403 responses indicating expired or revoked tokens, and 429 rate limiting (the runs endpoints allow 30 requests per minute) — and validate each cycle by diffing policy sets, teams and workspace access against the approved baseline
steps.audit_note: Failed collections must alert within the day, because the 14-day audit trail retention window makes late re-collection unrecoverable beyond two weeks

L4.title: HCP Terraform Continuous Evidence Pipeline with Webhooks and Audit Trail Ingestion

steps.action: Run a continuous audit trail ingester that polls GET https://app.terraform.io/api/v2/organization/audit-trail on a short interval (e.g. every 15 minutes) with a persisted since cursor, forwarding events to the SIEM; HCP Terraform offers no push or streaming delivery for this endpoint, so cursor-based polling is the supported continuous pattern
steps.permissions_required: Audit trail token; SIEM administrator for the ingestion pipeline
steps.security_note: Store the audit trail token in the pipeline's secrets manager and scope SIEM index access to the security and compliance teams
steps.audit_note: Continuous ingestion is the only way to preserve audit trail events beyond the platform's 14-day retention; requires the Standard or Premium edition

steps.action: Add real-time run event capture by creating a generic webhook notification per workspace via POST /api/v2/workspaces/:workspace_id/notification-configurations with destination-type generic and triggers run:created, run:planning, run:needs_attention, run:applying, run:completed and run:errored, pointed at your collector endpoint
steps.permissions_required: Workspace Admin, or an organisation API token
steps.audit_note: Webhook payloads arrive at each run state transition, giving an independent near-real-time change feed alongside the polled audit trail

steps.action: Configure the collector to verify the X-TFE-Notification-Signature header on every webhook delivery — an HMAC-SHA512 of the payload using the notification token you set — and reject unsigned or mismatched requests before ingesting into the SIEM
steps.security_note: HMAC verification prevents forged run events from polluting the evidence stream; keep the notification token in the collector's secret store
steps.audit_note: Signature-verified events carry integrity assurance that screenshots and manual exports cannot provide

steps.action: Reconcile the three sources continuously in the SIEM: webhook run events against the daily runs API snapshots (GET /api/v2/organizations/:organization_name/runs) and against audit trail entries, flagging any run visible in one feed but missing from another
steps.audit_note: Cross-source reconciliation turns three independent feeds into a completeness claim for the change record

steps.action: Publish completeness metrics and alerts: audit events ingested per day, cursor lag against the 14-day retention window, webhook delivery failures per workspace, runs applied without a matching approval event, and policy set or team access changes detected outside change windows
steps.audit_note: Cursor lag alerting well inside the 14-day retention window is the key control against unrecoverable audit trail gaps

steps.action: Generate an automated quarterly collection health report reconciling SIEM run counts against the workspace Runs pages and state version serials, recording coverage per workspace and any gaps with their remediation
steps.audit_note: Independent reconciliation against state version lineage validates that every infrastructure change is represented in the evidence archive
